response:
In C# it is possible to measure the time it takes for a UDP packet to reach a server  
but this is not straightforward  
UDP is connectionless and does not guarantee delivery  
so there is no built in way to know if the packet was received  
however you can use the SendAsync method and track the time  
you can also use the Timestamp option in the Socket class  
this allows you to get the time the packet was sent  
you can then compare it with the time it was received  
but this requires the server to send a response  
so it is not a one way measurement  
you can also use the Ping class but it measures round trip time  
not one way time  
so to measure one way time you need to implement your own logic  
you can send a packet and wait for a response  
then calculate the difference between the send and receive times  
this will give you an estimate of the one way delay  
but it is not accurate  
you can also use the Stopwatch class to measure the time  
between sending and receiving the packet  
this is a simple approach but may not be reliable  
in conclusion measuring one way UDP latency is possible but not easy  
it requires careful implementation and may not be accurate  
you can use the SendAsync method and track the time  
but you must account for network delays and packet loss
